title: Focus Trap

statement.markdown:
# Focus Trap Challenge

Focus trap is a situation where a user cannot navigate away from an element because it has a focus and the tabindex attribute set to a lower value than other elements. In this challenge, you will be given a simple form and will need to make sure that the user can navigate away from it using the keyboard.

### Requirements

1. Give the form a tabindex of 0 so it can receive focus.
2. Use the `autofocus` attribute to automatically give focus to the form when the page loads.
3. Ensure that pressing the `tab` key does not stay focused on the form.
4. Ensure that pressing the `escape` key resets the focus to the page's default element.

constraints: Use HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to solve this challenge.
Do not use any external libraries.

starterCode.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>focus-trap</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">
</head>
<body>
    <form id="myForm" tabindex="0">
        <label for="name">Name:</label>
        <input type="text" id="name" name="name">
        <button>Submit</button>
    </form>
    <button id="resetFocus">Reset Focus</button>
    <script src="script.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

gradingCriteria.description: The form automatically receives focus when the page loads.
Pressing the tab key does not stay focused on the form.
Pressing the escape key resets the focus to the page's default element.
